preset_description: Dark = substance signal. Spotlights product capabilities and metrics. CTA and pricing are understated — features are the story, not conversion pressure.

brief.audience: AI-first, intent-aware design system for founding teams

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for founding teams evaluating MODE as their intent-aware design system. At the decision stage, the Validator archetype needs immediate clarity on what this is and who it's for before weighing proof.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators require trust signals early. Showing customer logos, testimonials, or authority markers from other founding teams satisfies their need for social validation before engaging with harder evidence.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and performance proof points give the Validator the quantitative justification they need to feel confident moving toward a purchase decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Pricing tiers and plan comparisons framed around the outcomes evidenced above let the Validator make a rational, informed choice between options.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately follows Decision per structural rules. A clear, high-commitment CTA prompts the Validator to act on the confidence built by the preceding proof and pricing clarity.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat provides secondary paths — contact sales, explore docs, or revisit evidence — ensuring Validators who aren't fully convinced still have a productive exit rather than a dead end.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes context for founding teams evaluating MODE as their intent-aware design system. At the decision stage, the Validator archetype needs immediate clarity on what this is and who it's for before weighing proof.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators require trust signals early. Showing customer logos, testimonials, or authority markers from other founding teams satisfies their need for social validation before engaging with harder evidence.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and performance proof points give the Validator the quantitative justification they need to feel confident moving toward a purchase decision.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Pricing tiers and plan comparisons framed around the outcomes evidenced above let the Validator make a rational, informed choice between options.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Immediately follows Decision per structural rules. A clear, high-commitment CTA prompts the Validator to act on the confidence built by the preceding proof and pricing clarity.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: At decision stage, 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' ensures focus on the conversion action without navigation distraction.
For conversion stage, 'use text-only: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.'

slots.eyebrow: For founding teams moving in code
slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the semantic intelligence layer that resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience for every archetype and funnel stage — without manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust signals, and 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity' while 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility.'

slots.quote: We were building variant after variant by hand — Mover flow, Validator flow, different CTAs per page. MODE collapsed all of that into one brief. The resolution engine just makes the right call. It's infrastructure we should have had from day one.

page.reasoning: Validator context requires 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.'

slots.headline: What teams report after deploying MODE

stats.label: Median time from install to first intent-resolved page output
Reduction in design-to-code handoff cycles for teams using the IA planner
Components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and behavioral tokens — ready on day one

page.reasoning: Validator archetype at decision stage requires 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list' with 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' to guide the conversion choice.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, token config, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever — no subscription, no lock-in.
features: Semantic resolution engine — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
IA planner + multi-variant generation (Mover, Validator, Explorer in parallel)
Palette map — visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage
Token dashboard with editable palette map and preset switching
Lifetime access — lives in your codebase, no runtime dependency

page.reasoning: Validator variant at decision stage should 'include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required')' to remove friction and establish credibility at the conversion moment.

slots.headline: The semantic layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: One purchase. Full ownership. Running in under an hour — configured once, extensible forever.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. No subscription. Fork it, extend it, own it.

page.reasoning: At conversion stage on a key page, 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
